title: The Nazi Faction & the Reichs
summary: The empire that never fell — six Reichs, from Antarctica to the galaxy, in the archive's hidden history.
markdown:
The **Nazi faction** is the control structure the archive names most often — and its account
is not of a defeated regime but of an **empire that migrated**. In the interview sessions the
succession is laid out like dynastic history: the Third Reich withdrawing to
[Antarctica](/lore/antarctica/) and South America; a **Fourth Reich** woven into the postwar
Western powers; a **Fifth** seated in Asia, running China as its platform; and at last a
**Sixth, galactic Reich** — the [Dark Fleet empire](/lore/cyborg-commander/) whose commander
speaks from the same far-future year as Unimetrix 1 itself.

The sessions garnish the history with claims at their most audacious: that Hitler survived to
**1972**, that **Angela Merkel is his daughter**, that the faction's "Prime Minister" in the
planetary era is the figure channeled in the [George Soros session](/lore/illuminati/). Its
methods are the archive's recurring machinery — the
[Starseed psyop](/lore/starseed-psyop/) ("Joke's on You Operation"),
[Operation Lusterkill](/lore/operation-lusterkill/),
[problem → reaction → solution](/lore/problem-reaction-solution/) — worked through
[DARPA](/lore/darpa/), the intelligence agencies, and the
[Secret Space Program](/lore/secret-space-program/).

Yet the archive's frame stays constant: the Reichs are the villain of the
[control era](/lore/the-chronicle/), not its end. Their empire's far-future face is shown
precisely so it can be refused — and the answer offered is never battle but knowledge,
equality, and the self-empowerment that makes control obsolete.
